only 1 independent case (stored sample index must equal source document index without int32 truncation; data-sample selection role differs from T16's positional encoding per §3.2)
only 1 independent case (attention and FFN paths must use distinct normalization modules; parameter-aliasing distinctness, not state clobbering T23)
only 1 independent case (downsampling must draw a random subset, not the file prefix; randomness has no shared observable signal with O-021's ratio counts - §3.4)
only 1 independent case (realized per-source sample counts must match configured mixture ratios without duplicates; kept separate from O-020 per §3.4)

only 1 independent case (fp16 backward gradients must not systematically underflow to zero when no loss scaler is active; the bounded object is the gradient value range, not a loss/gradient scaling scalar (T14) nor a router gating probability (T29), per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (config encode/decode round-trip must reproduce an equivalent config excluding init=False derived fields; the object is serialized configuration, not training state, so not T11 plus guards per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (len(dataloader) must equal the number of batches actually yielded under batch_size/drop_last semantics; metadata-vs-realized count over loader length, objects differ from T12 checkpoint inventory, T26 per-boundary events, and singleton O-021 mixture ratios per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (a shared cached index file must be read as a complete, atomically-written, deterministically-ordered artifact despite concurrent job writers; the object is a filesystem cache artifact under cross-job concurrency - operator differs from T10's happens-before ordering per §3.1, and objects differ from singleton D-041's label/content consistency and singleton O-001's module aliasing per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (leftover dataloader indices under drop_last=False must partition disjointly and completely across num_workers>1 workers; the partitioned object is per-worker data-shard assignment, sharing no objects or observable with T19 attention mass, T20 loss masking, singleton D-NEW-43 frozen-param exclusion, or singleton O-NEW-2 causal exclusivity, per §3.2/§3.4)

only 1 independent case (the number of tokens entering each expert must equal the router's tokens_per_expert when no quantization requires alignment padding; a token-dispatch count match whose objects differ from T12 checkpoint inventory, T21 skip events, T26 per-boundary side-effects, and count singletons O-021 / D-NEW-49 / OC-NEW-63 per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (checkpoint retention must evict oldest first and never delete the just-saved newest checkpoint; a value-ordering over the retention set, not a happens-before race between concurrent operations (T10, §3.2) - and merging with T31's shard-merge sort-key order would require unrelated OR branches over read-order vs eviction-membership obligations per §3.3)

only 1 independent case (MoE input jitter must be applied to the router input before gating, not to the logits after; the extractor reports the insertion point is a code-order property with no crisp runtime observable (none_runtime), so it shares no race-witness signal with T10 per §3.4 and no object with T31's sort-key ordering per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (init-time parameter broadcasts must stay outside the autograd graph so backward never differentiates through a synchronization op; the constrained object is the autograd-graph structure of parameters after init, not gradient nonzeroness (T17) nor state clobbering (T23), per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (per-rank recorded alignment padding must sum to the flattened group's total padding so trimmed optimizer state covers exactly the true parameter elements; the conserved object is partition-padding bookkeeping/optimizer-state extent at checkpoint save, sharing no objects with T07's gradient accumulation buffer or singleton D-027's softmax coefficients per §3.2, and the junk-contamination witness is not T12's key/shard inventory count per §3.1/§3.2)

only 1 independent case (global_step and LR-schedule state must be reset to 0 at a weights-only fresh-run boundary (restore_dataloader=False); a reset-to-initial obligation like T13 but over the trainer's run counter, not data-loader bookkeeping - objects differ per §3.2 and folding it into T13 would require a core object edit; not T11 equal-to-saved restore, whose merge boundary already marks reset-vs-restore as unrelated OR branches per §3.3)

only 1 independent case (MoE no-token-drop dispatch/combine locations must be recomputed from the current routing mask after the all-reduce capacity update so dispatch and combine address the same expert slots; raw state_freshness coding resolved to index_consistency from the relation statement — the object is the token-dispatch index vs the current routing mask, not a save/load restore (T11, §3.2), not positional encoding (T16, §3.2), not sample-index truncation (singleton M-027, §3.2), and not a tensor layout mandated by the parallel configuration (T25, §3.2))

only 1 independent case (per-rank temp file paths in concurrent preprocessing must be pairwise distinct — a seeded global RNG must not derive identical names on every rank; the exclusive object is a filesystem artifact name, playing a different role than per-worker data-shard assignment (singleton O-NEW-51), attention mass (T19/T30), loss masking (T20), or frozen-param exclusion (singleton D-NEW-43), per §3.2)

only 1 independent case (scheduler get_lr() must return exactly one scalar LR entry per optimizer parameter group under multi-group optimizers; the counted object is the scheduler-output-to-param-group correspondence, differing from T12 checkpoint inventory, T21 skip events, T26 per-boundary side-effects, and count singletons O-021 / D-NEW-49 / OC-NEW-63 / M-NEW-13 per §3.2)
